rules.title: SELECT * in non-prepare models
rules.description: SELECT * in curated/enriched models passes all columns downstream, increasing read_bytes for every PBI query.
rules.recommendation: Explicitly list only the columns needed by downstream consumers.

rules.title: Missing liquid clustering on large curated tables
rules.description: Large incremental curated tables without liquid clustering force full file scans.  Clustering on common filter columns (e.g. date_key) enables data skipping.
rules.recommendation: Add liquid_clustered_by to the contract YAML with the primary filter columns.

rules.title: Serve view wider than PBI model needs
rules.description: Serve views that expose significantly more columns than the PBI model consumes increase bytes read per query.  PBI DirectQuery selects from the serve view, so extra columns are scanned and discarded.
rules.recommendation: Reduce the serve view SELECT to only columns used in the PBI model.

rules.title: No WHERE filter on serve view over large fact table
rules.description: Serve views over large fact tables with no WHERE clause expose the entire table to every PBI DirectQuery.  Even with liquid clustering, the absence of a date filter means PBI queries must scan the full history.
rules.recommendation: Add date-based WHERE filter to restrict the scan window.

rules.title: Functions on filter columns
rules.description: Wrapping filter columns in functions (UPPER, CAST, DATE_FORMAT, etc.) prevents predicate pushdown to the storage layer, disabling data skipping and cluster pruning.
rules.recommendation: Remove functions from filter columns; pre-compute values in the model instead.

rules.title: OR in JOIN conditions
rules.description: OR conditions in JOIN ON clauses prevent the optimiser from using hash joins, falling back to nested-loop joins which are significantly slower on large tables.
rules.recommendation: Refactor to separate JOINs or use UNION ALL of individual equality joins.

rules.title: ROW_NUMBER subselect instead of QUALIFY
rules.description: Using ROW_NUMBER() in a subquery to filter rows adds an extra materialisation step.  Databricks supports QUALIFY, which applies the window filter inline without the extra subquery.
rules.recommendation: Replace the ROW_NUMBER subquery pattern with QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER(...) = 1.

rules.title: materialized: table in enriched/curated
rules.description: Models materialised as 'table' are fully rebuilt on every dbt run, which is expensive for large datasets.  'incremental' materialisation only processes new/changed rows.
rules.recommendation: Switch to incremental materialisation with an appropriate strategy (merge/append).

rules.title: Missing incremental_predicates on large merge
rules.description: Merge operations without incremental_predicates scan the entire target table to find matching rows.  For tables with >100M rows, this causes slow dbt runs and warehouse contention.
rules.recommendation: Add incremental_predicates with a date-based predicate to limit the merge scan.

rules.title: UNION instead of UNION ALL
rules.description: UNION performs an implicit DISTINCT, adding a sort/hash aggregate step.  If deduplication is not required, UNION ALL avoids this overhead.
rules.recommendation: Replace UNION with UNION ALL unless deduplication is explicitly needed.

rules.title: Nested subqueries >2 levels
rules.description: Deeply nested subqueries increase query plan complexity and can prevent the Databricks optimiser from applying join reordering and predicate pushdown.
rules.recommendation: Refactor nested subqueries into CTEs (WITH clauses) for clarity and optimisation.

rules.title: Plain Python UDFs
rules.description: Python UDFs disable Photon vectorised execution and force row-at-a-time processing through the Python interpreter, dramatically increasing query latency.
rules.recommendation: Replace Python UDFs with native SQL/Spark functions where possible.

rules.title: Hardcoded magic numbers in SQL
rules.description: Magic numbers in WHERE clauses reduce readability and increase the risk of errors when business rules change.  They do not directly impact performance.
rules.recommendation: Use dbt variables or named constants for business-rule thresholds.

rules.title: Missing contract on public model
rules.description: Serve-layer models without contracts lack governance metadata (owner, lifecycle, alias).  This makes it harder to track ownership and manage breaking changes.
rules.recommendation: Create a contract YAML in _contracts/ with materialisation, alias, owner, and lifecycle.

rules.title: Serve view that should be materialised_view
rules.description: Serve views over large DirectQuery tables are re-evaluated on every PBI query.  A materialised view pre-computes the result, reducing latency for frequently queried tables.
rules.recommendation: Change materialisation from 'view' to 'materialized_view' in the contract YAML.

ruleResults.title: Functions on filter columns
ruleResults.recommendation: Remove functions from filter columns; pre-compute values in the model instead.
